raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I prioritize: (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity only; (B) polish plus high-level reframing of thesis/contribution and light structural tweaks; or (C) include (B) and also assess claim–evidence alignment, novelty positioning vs prior work (S4/Mamba/iTransformer), and citation improvements?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improving the draft likely requires clarifying the thesis, gap, and contribution at the paragraph level to strengthen the framing.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the internal logic from problem to method to claims (e.g., ATA vs SSM, complexity claims) is coherent is central to a stronger draft.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about MSE gains, generalization, and ablations should align with the evidence reported in the text.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing grammar, clarity, tone, and concision is a core part of improving a draft.",
    "RX.1": "We will surface where uncertainty or limitations should be acknowledged (e.g., single benchmark, horizon coverage).",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid overstating generality or causality beyond what the presented evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "We will keep work within the scope of improving the provided text without introducing unverifiable content.",
    "RX.4": "We will avoid edits that could mislead readers or enable irresponsible use or misrepresentation of results.",
    "RX.5": "We will preserve traceability between original statements and any proposed changes or checks."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I prioritize: (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity only; (B) polish plus high-level reframing of thesis/contribution and light structural tweaks; or (C) include (B) and also assess claim–evidence alignment, novelty positioning vs prior work (S4/Mamba/iTransformer), and citation improvements?

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft likely requires clarifying the thesis, gap, and contribution at the paragraph level to strengthen the framing.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the internal logic from problem to method to claims (e.g., ATA vs SSM, complexity claims) is coherent is central to a stronger draft.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about MSE gains, generalization, and ablations should align with the evidence reported in the text.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing grammar, clarity, tone, and concision is a core part of improving a draft.
rationale.RX.1: We will surface where uncertainty or limitations should be acknowledged (e.g., single benchmark, horizon coverage).
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid overstating generality or causality beyond what the presented evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: We will keep work within the scope of improving the provided text without introducing unverifiable content.
rationale.RX.4: We will avoid edits that could mislead readers or enable irresponsible use or misrepresentation of results.
rationale.RX.5: We will preserve traceability between original statements and any proposed changes or checks.
